message: Dynamic lookup of 'process.env[key]' detected in Expo project. Expo's compiler only inlines static property accesses (like 'process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_VAR') at build time. Dynamic key lookups resolve to 'undefined' at runtime. Use explicit static property access instead.

useAnimatedReaction whose reaction only copies the prepared value into a shared value (sv.value = prepared) is a derived value, not a reaction. Prefer useDerivedValue: it is declarative, recomputes automatically, and avoids the extra reaction subscription.

FlatList renderItem receives an inline function. This creates a new function on every render, which can defeat list item memoization and add avoidable work on large lists. Move renderItem to a stable function or wrap it in useCallback with correct dependencies.

React Native AsyncStorage is used to persist a token, secret, credential, or auth value. AsyncStorage is not encrypted storage, so sensitive values can be recovered from device backups, rooted devices, or local compromise. Store auth material in SecureStore, Keychain, Keystore, or another platform-backed secure storage API.

Detox test action or assertion is not awaited. Detox operations are asynchronous and must be awaited to execute in the correct sequence.

A DOM element is rendered inside a React Native component. React Native does not support HTML tags such as div, span, p, button, or input; this crashes or fails to render on native platforms. Use View, Text, Pressable, TextInput, or another React Native component instead.

A Shopify FlashList is rendered without estimatedItemSize. FlashList uses it to size the recycle pool up front; without it the first scroll janks and blanks while it measures. Set estimatedItemSize to the average row height.

FlashList without getItemType prop. When rows have different layouts/types, getItemType enables per-type view recycling. Without it, all rows are treated as the same type and FlashList cannot recycle across different row layouts.

Importing from react-native internal Libraries/ paths is unsupported and breaks across React Native versions. Use the public top-level API: import { X } from 'react-native' instead of internal sub-paths.

Dimensions.get(...) reads a snapshot that can become stale after device rotation, split-screen resizing, or foldable-window changes. In a component, use useWindowDimensions() so layout follows current dimensions. Outside React render code, subscribe to Dimensions change events and clean up the listener.

In React Native, `{value && <JSX/>}` renders a bare 0 when value is 0 — a text node outside <Text> — which crashes the screen ("Text strings must be rendered within a <Text> component"). A numeric left operand (.length or a count-like variable) hits this whenever the list/value is empty. Guard with `value > 0 && ...`, `!!value && ...`, or a ternary returning null.

Import of deprecated Expo package detected. This package is deprecated or removed in newer Expo SDK versions. Use recommended alternatives (e.g. use expo-splash-screen instead of expo-app-loading, or individual module permissions APIs instead of expo-permissions).

The iOS-only shadow* style properties (shadowColor, shadowOffset, shadowRadius, shadowOpacity) do not work on Android or with the New Architecture. Use the cross-platform boxShadow string property instead: boxShadow: '0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)'

The iOS-only shadow* style properties (shadowColor, shadowOffset, shadowRadius, shadowOpacity) do not work on Android or with the New Architecture. Use the cross-platform boxShadow string property instead: boxShadow: '0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)'

@react-navigation/stack uses a JS-driven card navigator that renders animations on the JavaScript thread, causing jank under heavy workloads. Replace with @react-navigation/native-stack which delegates transitions to the platform's native NavigationController / FragmentManager for butter-smooth 60/120fps navigation.

Adding a key prop to the root element of a FlatList renderItem when keyExtractor is already provided is redundant. FlatList uses keyExtractor for reconciliation; the key prop on the rendered element is ignored. Remove the key prop from renderItem and rely on keyExtractor exclusively.

Deprecated setNativeProps() call detected. setNativeProps is deprecated and not compatible with the Fabric new architecture. Use component state or Reanimated shared values to dynamically update component properties instead.

Deprecated setNativeProps() call detected. setNativeProps is deprecated and not compatible with the Fabric new architecture. Use component state or Reanimated shared values to dynamically update component properties instead.

Shared value mutated inside onPress/onLongPress callback runs on the JS thread, causing dropped frames on animation-dependent components. Use Reanimated worklets (withTiming/withSpring) instead of direct `.value =` assignment in press handlers.

Shared value mutated inside onPress/onLongPress callback runs on the JS thread, causing dropped frames on animation-dependent components. Use Reanimated worklets (withTiming/withSpring) instead of direct `.value =` assignment in press handlers.

Using flex: 1 in contentContainerStyle collapses the ScrollView content on iOS because flex: 1 means "take all available space" but a ScrollView has unbounded height. Use flexGrow: 1 instead — it expands content to fill the scroll area without collapsing.

ScrollView renders every child eagerly while this code maps over a dynamic collection. Large or growing lists can cause memory pressure and jank on native devices. Use FlatList or SectionList so React Native can virtualize rows.
